title: Aluminum Prices Today 2026 | Live Chart, Spot & Futures Data
summary: Aluminum prices advanced 2.08% to $3,568.6/ton globally, with Chinese spot markets at ¥24,255/ton. Global aluminum edged up 0.10% to $3,596.04/ton amid low LME inventories and Strait of Hormuz blockade threats, though easing tensions capped rallies. China's production is constrained near 45-million-ton capacity with sluggish demand ahead of Labour Day.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum rose to 3,578.45 USD/T on May 11, 2026, up 2.15% from the previous day, despite a 1.33% monthly decline. UK aluminum futures rebounded to $3,530 per tonne from a three-week low amid expectations of prolonged Middle East supply disruptions.

title: Aluminum Price (Monthly) - Historical Data & Trends
summary: Aluminum price reached 3599.85, up 6.73% from last month (3372.95) and 35.42% from one year ago (2658.29), reflecting ongoing upward trends in the market.

title: Global Primary Aluminum Output Hits Historic High!
summary: Primary aluminum production reached a record-breaking high in October 2024, according to the International Aluminum Institute (IAI). The surge is driven by global demand and economic recovery.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: The country's largest aluminum producer is boosting production to full capacity for the first time in about a decade. Century Aluminum made this decision amid President Donald Trump's tariffs.

title: Iran's aluminum output hits record
summary: Iran reported record aluminum output of 169,141 metric tons in the three months to June 20, representing a 1.5% increase from the same quarter the previous year. The increase was driven by upgrade programs at smelters and improved feedstock supply.

title: Alcoa sets production records at five smelters
summary: Alcoa achieved annual aluminum production of 2,319 thousand metric tons in 2025, representing a 5% increase compared to 2024. The company set production records at five of its smelters.

title: West Asia Has 10% Global Aluminium Capacity, Most Remains Under Threat
summary: West Asia holds 10% of global aluminum capacity, though most of it remains under threat. Aluminum prices are at 4-year highs and may outperform the broader metals sector.

title: Can America smelt again?
summary: Domestic primary aluminum production in the United States has plummeted from approximately 1.6 million tonnes to 700,000 tonnes over the past decade—a 57% decline. Rising energy costs and international competition have decimated domestic capacity, leaving America increasingly dependent on foreign suppliers.

title: Green Aluminum is Competitive Today. It's Time to Start Transforming
summary: Green, recycled aluminum may cut into margins but can be produced with less than 5% of the carbon footprint of primary aluminum. BloombergNEF's in-depth report examines technologies and costs for decarbonizing the aluminum sector, the first in a series on near-zero emissions industrial materials.

title: Out Of The Woods Expands Its Sustainable Lineup with New Coastal Collection
summary: Out of the Woods®, a sustainable lifestyle brand, has expanded its lineup with a new Coastal Collection. This move highlights ongoing innovations in sustainable products within the environmental sector.

title: Aluminium Bottle Market to Reach USD 10,784.4 Million by 2035 as Sustainable Premium Packaging Accelerates Across Beverage and Personal Care Industries
summary: The global Aluminium Bottle Market is projected to grow from USD 7,794.6 million in 2025 to USD 10,784.4 million by 2035 at a CAGR of 3.3%. This growth is driven by accelerating demand for sustainable premium packaging in beverage and personal care industries.

title: Zero-carbon aluminium is the future of aluminium production
summary: Hydro has reduced emissions by 55% since 1990 and aims for zero-carbon primary aluminum production to meet growing demand sustainably. Their world-leading electrolysis technology is positioned for carbon capture, supporting the green transition as aluminum demand rises 70-80% by 2050, largely via recycling.

title: Steel Price Update: Rose to 3,241 CNY/T on May 12, 2026
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,241 CNY/T on May 12, 2026, up 0.12% from the previous day, with a 5.23% increase over the past month. Steel rebar futures climbed toward CNY 3,250 per ton amid expectations for stronger demand, stable production in China, and improved mill profit margins. Stronger economic data and surging exports further supported prices.

title: Steel Falls to 3,226 CNY/T on May 12, 2026
summary: Steel prices fell to 3,226 CNY/T on May 12, 2026, down 0.34% from the previous day, though up 4.74% over the past month. Steel rebar futures surged to around CNY 3,250 per ton after the May Day holiday, bolstered by robust buying and a seven-week drawdown in inventories indicating tightening supply.

title: HRC Steel Falls to 1,121.10 USD/T on May 11, 2026
summary: HRC Steel decreased to 1,121.10 USD/T on May 11, 2026, down 0.79% from the previous day, but recently hit 1100.00 USD/T, the highest since January 2024. Over the past four weeks, prices gained 3.79%, reflecting positive trends in hot-rolled coil steel.

title: US Mills' Capability Utilization Hits 81.4% Last Week
summary: US steel mills' capability utilization reached 81.4% last week, up from 80.4% the prior week and 76.6% a year ago, with raw steel output continuing to rise. The May scrap market settled with mixed prime tags after a prolonged process. SSAB increased transaction prices for plate orders shipping from June 28, and Nucor hiked spot HR prices by $10/ton.

title: EU Mills Sustain Steel Prices Through Trade Barriers
summary: EU mills are maintaining steel prices via trade barriers rather than stronger demand, as weak manufacturing sentiment persists amid geopolitical tensions, US tariffs, and CBAM uncertainty. Italian distributors reported lower sales and prices in April year-over-year for long and flat products. Concerns rise ahead of revised EU safeguard measures effective July 1, reducing import quotas by 47%.

title: Copper futures remained above $5.95 per pound on Friday after rebounding in the previous session
summary: Copper futures stayed above $5.95 per pound following a rebound, bolstered by signs of improving demand in China. This comes despite ongoing headwinds from the Middle East conflict. The price rose to 6.46 USD/Lbs on May 12, up 0.74% from the previous day.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco announced that its smelter at the El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday following a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The news comes amid rising copper prices.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel prices rose to 19,194 USD/T on May 11, up 1.31% from the previous day, reaching the highest since June 2024 amid supply disruptions. Futures traded around $19,000 per tonne after pulling back from a two-year high near $19,600 due to profit-taking, supported by Indonesia's mining quota cuts and a sulfur shortage from Strait of Hormuz disruptions. Over the past month, prices have gained 8.26% and 23.43% year-over-year.

title: Nickel Prices Today 2026 | Live Chart, Battery Grade & Stainless Steel
summary: Global nickel prices advanced 1.38% to $19,204.64 per ton, with Chinese prices at ¥130,528 per ton, driven by supply constraints. Indonesia slashed mining quotas, tightening ore availability, while a global sulfur shortage from Middle East tensions threatens MHP processing. A weaker US dollar and firm SHFE quotes provide further bullish support.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel rose to 19,194 USD/T on May 11, 2026, up 1.31% from the previous day, marking the highest since June 2024. Nickel futures traded above $19,500 per tonne, nearing a two-year high, driven by supply disruptions and tightening raw material availability. Over the past 4 weeks, prices gained 10.59%, with a 22.15% increase over the last 12 months.

title: Freeport now aims for 65% capacity in the second half of 2026
summary: Freeport aims to reach 65% production capacity in the second half of 2026, 80% by mid-2027, and near full capacity by the end of 2027. This update pertains to nickel production capacity expansion. The announcement reflects ongoing efforts to ramp up output amid market dynamics.
